text.source_text: भेदव्यपदेशश्चेह भवति — ‘ तमेवैकं जानथ आत्मानम् ’ इति ज्ञेयज्ञातृभावेन । तत्र प्राणभृत् तावन्मुमुक्षुत्वाज्ज्ञाता । परिशेषादात्मशब्दवाच्यं ब्रह्म ज्ञेयं द्युभ्वाद्यायतनमिति गम्यते , न प्राणभृत् ॥ ५ ॥
text.transliteration_iast: bhedavyapadeśaśceha bhavati — ‘ tamevaikaṃ jānatha ātmānam ’ iti jñeyajñātṛbhāvena | tatra prāṇabhṛt tāvanmumukṣutvājjñātā | pariśeṣādātmaśabdavācyaṃ brahma jñeyaṃ dyubhvādyāyatanamiti gamyate , na prāṇabhṛt || 5 ||

translation.literal_translation: And here there is also explicit statement of difference: 'Know that one alone as the Self' is said in the relation of known and knower. There, the life-bearer, as one desiring liberation, is the knower. By residual elimination it is understood that Brahman, denoted by the word Self, is the knowable support of heaven and earth, not the life-bearer.
translation.technical_translation: Sutra 1.3.5 bhashya: jneya-jnatṛ distinction in the Muṇḍaka sentence blocks identity of seeker-jiva with the designated ayatana; parisesa yields Brahman as the object-term.
translation.interpretive_note: Uses epistemic role-separation (knower vs known) as referential test.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Tracks each block inside the section-level inferential sequence

uncertainty_notes: Curated in contiguous S03 pass with S04-style inferential framing.
A later philological pass may refine precision around kṣetrajña/upādhi and two-birds imagery terms.
